title: Stelis erucosa
intro: Stelis erucosa, commonly known as Stelis, is a flowering plant species in the orchid family (Orchidaceae) that is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Stelis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Stelis is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Stelis erucosa is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest family of flowering plants. Stelis erucosa is further classified within the subtribe Pleurothallidinae, a group of orchids commonly known as the "spoil orchids." The species is native to South America, where it can be found in a variety of habitats. Stelis erucosa is a terrestrial orchid, meaning it grows in the ground rather than in trees.
